linux-gfx90a.blocked_reason: ported + GPU-validated. liboneflow.so (1.3GB) and the _oneflow_internal pybind module LINK; the ep/cuda/primitive slice GPU-validates vs PyTorch-ROCm (device='cuda' == HIP backend, fwd+bwd): test_add/cast/where/masked_fill/broadcast_ops/transpose/softmax/layer_norm = 64 passed, matmul core = 9 passed. Monolith built with the ROCm clang; hip::host+roc::rccl linked (not hip::device); 17 .cu deferred (cuDNN/cuBLASLt/cuSOLVER/CUTLASS+wmma/libcu++/P2P) with their host registrations guarded or stubbed (NewLruCache, GridSampleKernelUtil<kCUDA> -> CPU-only). GPU faults fixed: hipblasSetMathMode no-op on CDNA, __launch_bounds__(1024) on the norm block kernels, CUDNN_BN_MIN_EPSILON=0.0, and the wave64 LayerNormParamGrad reduction (tile/launch/shared/width all 32, width-confined shfl). Fork 68718f0.

linux-gfx1100.blocked_reason: GPU-validated on gfx1100 (RDNA3, wave32, ROCm 7.2.1). Fresh build from fork HEAD 68718f03: 190 gfx1100 code objects, OF_GPU_WARP_SIZE=32. Test slice: 73 passed / 2 failed (same pre-existing artifacts as gfx90a -- numpy __bool__ dtype-interop, not GPU faults). Wave32 warp reductions: softmax/layer_norm/rms_norm all produce correct results (softmax max_abs_diff=1.49e-08, layer_norm bwd gamma/beta no NaN, rms_norm bwd weight no NaN). LayerNormParamGrad wave64 fix is correct on wave32 (tile=32, block_dim_x=32, width=32 matches native wavefront). No fork changes needed. Fork 68718f0.
